thought.text:
Inlining worked. Now `func` was inlined. Body simplified to `%add = add %tmp.0, %i.0`. The 60 calls per outer iter became 60 adds, then `tmp / 60`. Even better would be to recognize `tmp = i*60` (since we add `i` 60 times in inner loop), then `tmp/60 = i`. That requires loop-induction analysis.

Anyway, big improvement. Let me run scoring:

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task4-score 2>&1 | tail -25 | head -22

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task4-score 2>&1 | tail -25 | head -22", "security_risk": "MEDIUM", "summary": "Score with inlining", "timeout": 600}
